sections.paragraph:
an unused root. Syr. ܚܨܳ Pael ܚܰܝܶܨ to in-close, to surround; comp. حاط, ܚܳܛ surround. Hence חַיִץ a wall, and-

חוץ

m. properly a wall; hence especially the outside (of a house), and thus מִבַּיִת וּמִחוּץ within and without (properly, on the house side, and the wall side) are often opposed to each other, Gen 6:14 Exo 25:11. Hence it is-

(1) subst. whatever is without-(a) out of the house, the street, Jer 37:21 Job 18:17 pl. חוּצֹות Job 5:10 Isa 5:25 10:6 .-(b) out of the city, the fields, country, deserts. Job 5:10 (Aram. בַּר), whence there are opposed to each other אֶרֶץ וְחוּצֹות Pro 8:26, the (tilled) earth and the desert regions, comp. Mar 1:45.

(2) adv. out of doors, without, abroad, Deu 23:14, e.g. מֹולֶדֶת־חוּץ born abroad, i.e. away from home, Lev 18:19 also, forth, forth abroad, Deu 23:13. So also with ה parag. חוּצָה without, on the outside, 1Ki 6:6 abroad, forth, to the outside, Exo 12:46, with art. הַחוּץ forth, Jud. 19:25 Neh 13:8 (prop. into the street), and הַחוּצָה Gen 15:5. With prepositions-(a) בַּחוּץ without (in the open place), Gen 9:22.-(b) לַחוּץ poet. id. Psa 41:7, and לַחוּצָה 2Ch 32:5.-(c) מִחוּץ without, on the outside, as opposed to מִבַּיִת within, Gen 6:14. מֵהַחוּץ id. Eze 41:25.-(d) מִחוּץ לְ without (in a state of rest, as opposed to motion), e.g. מִחוּץ לָעִיר without (or outside the city), Gen 19:16 24:11 . מִחוּצָה לְ Eze 40:40, 44 Eze 40:44.-(e) אֶל־מִחוּץ לְ without (after verbs of motion), Num 5:3, , אֶל־מִחוּץ לַמַּחֲנֶה “without the camp;” Deu 23:11 Lev 4:12. Metaph.-(f) חוּץ מִן besides, Ecc 2:25. (So Ch. בַּר מִן, Syr., Sam. and Zab. ܠܒܰܪ ܡܶܢ.) Hence הִיצֹון.
